skill.name: cold-email
skill.description: Helps write concise B2B cold emails, subject lines, personalization, CTAs, and follow-up sequences for outbound sales.
skill.summary: Write concise B2B cold outreach emails and follow-up sequences.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by sales and marketing terms in documentation and eval text. No evidence found of cryptography, command execution, network access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration. The skill does request reading an optional local product marketing context file, so it carries low filesystem context risk.

low_findings.title: Local Context File Read
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read optional local product marketing context files before asking questions. This is legitimate for tailoring output, but users should avoid placing secrets in those context files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read instruction is explicit and limited to two named markdown files. No evidence found that the content is sent to a network endpoint or command.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged wording in cold email examples and sales frameworks as weak cryptography. The cited files contain marketing guidance, eval assertions, and copywriting framework names, not cryptographic algorithms or code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are prose or JSON eval text for cold-email behavior. No encryption, hashing, cipher configuration, or executable crypto code appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged email phrases such as checking in, inbox, LinkedIn, and avoidance guidance as reconnaissance. These are sales-copy examples and anti-patterns, not host, network, or account discovery instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The nearby text discusses cold-email writing quality and phrases to avoid. No evidence found of system probing, port scanning, enumeration, or network discovery.

low_findings.title: Network Library Static Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged SKILL.md line 51 as a Python HTTP library indicator. The line uses the word requests to describe meeting requests in CTA guidance, not network code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain markdown prose about sales calls to action. No import, URL, socket, HTTP client, or outbound request appears there.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner twice flagged SKILL.md line 15 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The backticks are markdown formatting around file paths, not executable command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is markdown documentation and contains no shell command, Ruby expression, or interpolation. It does contain a benign local file read instruction, recorded separately as low filesystem risk.

content.value_statement: Cold outreach often fails because it sounds generic, pushy, or too long. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create concise emails with clear value, relevant personalization, and low-friction asks.
actual_capabilities: Writes first-touch B2B cold emails from audience, offer, proof, and research inputs.
Creates subject lines that are short, clear, and aligned with the email body.
Builds three to five email follow-up sequences with changing angles and timing.
Reviews weak outreach copy and explains specific problems before rewriting it.
Adapts tone for executives, technical buyers, founders, and mid-level operators.
limitations: It does not send emails, manage inboxes, or connect to outreach platforms.
It needs accurate audience, offer, proof, and research inputs for strong output.
It does not verify prospect data or legal compliance for each jurisdiction.
It is focused on cold outbound, not lifecycle nurture or customer newsletters.

use_cases.title: Draft outbound campaigns
use_cases.description: Create concise first-touch emails and follow-ups for a specific buyer, pain point, and proof point.

use_cases.title: Improve founder-led outreach
use_cases.description: Turn a founder pitch into a peer-level message that asks for a simple reply.

use_cases.title: Audit low-performing emails
use_cases.description: Diagnose weak subject lines, vague value claims, poor personalization, and high-friction CTAs.

prompt_templates.title: Write a first cold email
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner draft from basic campaign inputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a cold email to [role] at [company type]. We help with [problem]. Our proof is [result]. Use a low-friction CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Create subject line options
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate short subject lines for one outreach angle.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 10 subject lines for this cold email. Keep them short, specific, and internal-looking. Avoid clickbait and fake urgency.

prompt_templates.title: Build a follow-up sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan multiple touches after no reply.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a four-email follow-up sequence for this first email. Each touch should add a new angle, proof point, or useful resource.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose and rewrite a campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this cold email campaign. Open rate is [rate] and reply rate is [rate]. Explain likely causes, then rewrite the sequence.

output_examples.input: Write a cold email to VP Marketing buyers for a content analytics product with a 3x revenue proof point.
output: Saw many B2B teams are publishing more but still guessing which articles create pipeline.
We help marketing teams connect content to revenue. Similar customers saw 3x more content-attributed revenue in 90 days.

output_examples.input: Create a follow-up after no reply to an HR software email.
output: One other angle: teams usually feel the cost of slow onboarding after the hiring plan is already underway.
Our HR platform helps managers spot where new hires stall before week four.
Would that be useful to see?

output_examples.input: Review an email that opens with "I hope this email finds you well" and asks for a 30-minute demo.
output_examples.output: The email leads with filler, talks about the sender, and asks for too much too soon. A stronger version should start with the buyer problem and ask for interest.

best_practices: Use one specific research signal that connects directly to the buyer problem.
Keep the first email short and use one clear ask.
Honor opt-outs and stop after the breakup email.
anti_patterns: Do not fake replies, forwards, urgency, or personal familiarity.
Do not send the same template with only a first name changed.
Do not ask for a long meeting before earning interest.

faq.question: Does this skill send cold emails?
faq.answer: No. It only helps draft, review, and structure cold email copy.

faq.question: Can it write follow-up sequences?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create three to five follow-ups with different angles and a final breakup email.

faq.question: Does it handle lifecycle email campaigns?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for unsolicited outbound outreach, not nurture campaigns for opted-in leads.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output?
faq.answer: Provide the buyer role, company type, problem, offer, proof point, and any relevant research signal.

faq.question: Can it improve low reply rates?
faq.answer: It can diagnose likely copy problems and rewrite the email, but results still depend on targeting and deliverability.

faq.question: Does it check legal compliance?
faq.answer: No. Users must confirm consent, opt-out, and regional email requirements before sending.
